programme.title: Highly Questionable
programme.desc: Veteran Miami Herald columnist and radio host Dan Le Batard leads a humorous, critical and sometimes controversial discussion of the hot sports topics of the day, from the Clevelander Hotel in Miami Beach. Known as a thoughtful and witty contrarian, Le Batard is not afraid to speak his mind and let his innate Cuban-American influences shine through, which is apparent in the fast-paced show. He's joined on each 30-minute episode by his father, Gonzalo "Papi" Le Batard.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: NBA: The Jump
programme.desc: "The Jump" is a daily NBA show hosted by Rachel Nichols. The fast-paced program originates from Los Angeles- across the street from where the Lakers and Clippers play- and features appearances by current and former players, as well as a rotating cast of ESPN analysts, reporters and insiders who provide commentary. "The Jump" is like having a fun NBA conversation with your friends in your living room, except your friends are Scottie Pippen and Tracy McGrady. The discussion encompasses all the fun, serious, smart, crazy big-issue, small-smile moments that make the NBA popular.

programme.desc: Veteran Miami Herald columnist and radio host Dan Le Batard leads a humorous, critical and sometimes controversial discussion of the hot sports topics of the day, from the Clevelander Hotel in Miami Beach. Known as a thoughtful and witty contrarian, Le Batard is not afraid to speak his mind and let his innate Cuban-American influences shine through, which is apparent in the fast-paced show. He's joined on each 30-minute episode by his father, Gonzalo "Papi" Le Batard.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: NBA: The Jump
programme.desc: "The Jump" is a daily NBA show hosted by Rachel Nichols. The fast-paced program originates from Los Angeles- across the street from where the Lakers and Clippers play- and features appearances by current and former players, as well as a rotating cast of ESPN analysts, reporters and insiders who provide commentary. "The Jump" is like having a fun NBA conversation with your friends in your living room, except your friends are Scottie Pippen and Tracy McGrady. The discussion encompasses all the fun, serious, smart, crazy big-issue, small-smile moments that make the NBA popular.

programme.title: Global 3000
programme.desc: Die Welt ist immer stärker miteinander verbunden und voneinander abhängig. Wie beeinflusst aber das tatsächlich das Leben? Die Sendung gibt der Globalisierung ein Gesicht und zeigt die Geschichten der Menschen, die jeden Tag die Folgen erleben.

programme.title: Markus Lanz
programme.desc: Markus Lanz lädt prominente Gäste und Experten aus allen Bereichen des öffentlichen Lebens zu seiner bunten Talkrunde ein. In der Regel sind es vier Gäste, die einzeln vorgestellt werden und ihre persönlichen Erfahrungen zu den Themen einbringen.

programme.desc: Neue Ideen, große Events und die wichtigsten Debatten im Bereich der Kultur.

programme.desc: Markus Lanz lädt prominente Gäste und Experten aus allen Bereichen des öffentlichen Lebens zu seiner bunten Talkrunde ein. In der Regel sind es vier Gäste, die einzeln vorgestellt werden und ihre persönlichen Erfahrungen zu den Themen einbringen.

programme.title: Fit und Gesund
programme.desc: Das Gesundheitsmagazin zeigt das Interessanteste aus den Bereichen Medizin und Wellness. Ob Schulmedizin oder alternative Heilmethoden, ob Ernährung und Fitness oder Schönheit und Wellness - gezeigt wird, was gesund und fit hält.

programme.title: Kick Off! - Der Bundesliga - Spieltag
programme.desc: Die aktuellsten Informationen von der Bundesliga.

programme.title: Hart aber fair
programme.desc: Die Diskussionssendung bereitet aktuelle politische, wirtschaftliche und gesellschaftliche Themen auf. Filme und Reportagen liefern dabei Hintergründe, bereichern die Diskussion mit harten Fakten und beleuchten das Thema aus unterschiedlichen Perspektiven. Die Zuschauer können sich mit ihren Meinungen, Fragen, Ängsten und Sorgen per Telefon, Internet, Fax oder E-Mail in die Sendung einmischen. Gäste aus Politik, Kultur, Wissenschaft und Wirtschaft werden anschließend mit den Meinungen der Zuschauer konfrontiert und zur Diskussion geladen.

programme.title: Kulturzeit
programme.desc: Das werktägliche Kulturmagazin mischt sich in kulturelle und gesellschaftspolitische Fragen ein. `Kulturzeit' bietet ergänzende Hintergrundinformationen, Porträts und Gespräche zu aktuellen und brisanten Fragen.

programme.title: Der Tag
programme.desc: Das Nachrichtenmagazin fasst die wichtigsten Ereignisse des Tages mit Blick auf das politische und gesellschaftliche Geschehen in Deutschland, Europa und der Welt noch einmal zusammen. Hintergrundanalysen mit Studiogästen ergänzen den Tagesrückblick.

programme.desc: Die Sendung `Auf den Punkt!' beschäftigt sich mit den wichtigsten Themen rund um Deutschlands Hauptstadt. Bekannte Politiker werden ins Studio eingeladen und reden offen, kritisch und ehrlich über wichtige Themen.

programme.title: Schätze der Welt - Erbe der Menschheit
programme.desc: Viele Naturphänomene und menschliche Kulturleistungen sind einzigartig und schützenswert. Die UNESCO schützt sie als Erbe der Menschheit. Die Sendereihe stellt die verschiedenen Welterbe-Stätten vor.

programme.title: Kulturzeit
programme.desc: Das werktägliche Kulturmagazin mischt sich in kulturelle und gesellschaftspolitische Fragen ein. `Kulturzeit' bietet ergänzende Hintergrundinformationen, Porträts und Gespräche zu aktuellen und brisanten Fragen.

programme.title: Kick off! Life - Fußball ist Leben
programme.desc: Die Sendung gibt Einblicke in die Welt des Fußballs.

programme.title: Maybrit Illner - Politik - Talkshow
programme.desc: Die Journalistin Maybrit Illner diskutiert schwierige und umstrittene Themen aus der Politik.

programme.title: Kulturzeit
programme.desc: Das werktägliche Kulturmagazin mischt sich in kulturelle und gesellschaftspolitische Fragen ein. `Kulturzeit' bietet ergänzende Hintergrundinformationen, Porträts und Gespräche zu aktuellen und brisanten Fragen.

programme.title: Fokus Europa
programme.desc: Fokus Europa, das neue ORF-Magazin, zu den aktuellen Hintergründen rund um die EU.

programme.title: Der Tag
programme.desc: Das Nachrichtenmagazin fasst die wichtigsten Ereignisse des Tages mit Blick auf das politische und gesellschaftliche Geschehen in Deutschland, Europa und der Welt noch einmal zusammen. Hintergrundanalysen mit Studiogästen ergänzen den Tagesrückblick.

programme.title: Fit und Gesund
programme.desc: Das Gesundheitsmagazin zeigt das Interessanteste aus den Bereichen Medizin und Wellness. Ob Schulmedizin oder alternative Heilmethoden, ob Ernährung und Fitness oder Schönheit und Wellness - gezeigt wird, was gesund und fit hält.

programme.title: Markus Lanz
programme.desc: Markus Lanz lädt prominente Gäste und Experten aus allen Bereichen des öffentlichen Lebens zu seiner bunten Talkrunde ein. In der Regel sind es vier Gäste, die einzeln vorgestellt werden und ihre persönlichen Erfahrungen zu den Themen einbringen.

Die Sendereihe berichtet aus außergewöhnlicher Perspektive über für unsere Gesellschaft typische Lebenssituationen: Die Kamera begleitet eine Gruppe oder einzelne Menschen in meist besonders exponierter sozialer Situation.

programme.title: Quick Pitch
programme.desc: Sometimes, fans of America's national pastime might miss a day or two of their favorite sport due to pesky things like work, a wedding anniversary, or a child's high-school graduation ceremony. Rest easy, devotees of the diamond -- MLB Network has you covered. "Quick Pitch" not only provides complete highlights of each game from the previous day, but also presents a glimpse at upcoming contests, including pitching matchups.

programme.title: MLB Central
programme.desc: MLB Network's first-ever regular-season morning show features hosts Robert Flores, Mark DeRosa and Lauren Shehadi reporting from Studio 21, named in honor of Hall of Famer and humanitarian Roberto Clemente. Updates and context on the latest news, extensive on-field highlights, and long-form conversations with guests, celebrities and insiders from around baseball are presented each weekday morning. Flores joined the network in 2016 as a host/reporter, DeRosa offers a unique outlook as a former player, and Shehadi honed crisp interviewing skills during three years of hosting MLB Network's "The Rundown."

programme.title: High Heat
programme.desc: Chris Russo has never been afraid to bring the heat as a radio host. Nicknamed "Mad Dog," he shows real passion for sports when the subject is baseball. Hearing him rant on satellite radio is one thing; seeing him is electrifying, which is why MLB Network collared Mad Dog to talk hardball each weekday. The hourlong studio show begins with Russo's monologue on the day's big headlines, then accelerates to league news with a roster of contributors including analysts Al Leiter, Dan Plesac, Harold Reynolds, Bill Ripken, insider Tom Verducci, and national/local beat writers and broadcasters.

programme.title: The Rundown
programme.desc: The title says it all, as any good title should. "The Rundown" presents a jump-start to all that's happening each weeknight in Major League Baseball, including highlights, live look-ins to games in progress, and access to batting practice. There's also team reports, injury updates and the latest news regarding roster moves. Matt Yallof and Lauren Shehadi host.

programme.title: MLB Now
programme.desc: The hourlong weekday afternoon "MLB Now" presents the day's top stories in baseball by spanning the sport's cultural divide. Host Brian Kenny discusses the issues and provides analysis, with a bent toward modern analytics to prove his points. The series also features interviews with personalities throughout the game, and viewer opinions are welcome as well via social media polls.

programme.title: Intentional Talk
programme.desc: MLB Network's first-ever talk show features some talk and a lot of banter each weekday for hosts Chris Rose and Kevin Millar, who discuss between themselves and with others associated with baseball what's hot and what's not in and around the national pastime. They also run out of the baseline at times when they welcome guests from the worlds of entertainment and pop culture. Rose is well known for batting topics around as the longtime host of Fox Sports' wacky "The Best Damn Sports Show Period," while Millar gained fame as a member of the 2004 world champion Boston Red Sox.

programme.title: MLB Tonight
programme.desc: When they're not tuning in to the games themselves, baseball fans know where to turn for their nightly baseball fix. MLB Network's signature program, "MLB Tonight" presents complete coverage of all of the games, beginning with the first pitch on the East Coast and ending with the final out on the West Coast. The Emmy-winning studio show features highlights, live look-ins and breaking news, plus analysis from a rotating roster that includes former players Eric Byrnes, Al Leiter, Dan Plesac, Billy Ripken and Mike Lowell, and inside information men Tom Verducci, Jon Heyman, Peter Gammons and Ken Rosenthal. Greg Amsinger, Brian Kenny and Matt Vasgersian are among the hosts.

programme.title: E! News: Daily Pop
programme.desc: "E! News' has been a staple of the cable network's evening lineup for years, and now the franchise is expanding to earlier in the day with `Daily Pop'. The broadcast offers the same type of behind-the-scenes information, news and celebrity interviews that viewers have come to expect from `E! News' but also adds other elements typical of morning shows. Hosts Catt Sadler, Justin Sylvester and Carissa Culiner lead what the network calls "fun, insightful and relatable conversations" revolving around the day"s biggest pop-culture stories.

programme.title: Revenge Body With Khloé Kardashian
programme.desc: Khloé sits down with people who are at their lowest point and puts them through the ultimate transformation using her handpicked team.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians
programme.desc: The girls take one last sister getaway to San Francisco before their babies arrive, but they quickly tire of Kourtney's bad attitude. Khloé anxiously awaits news of her baby's gender.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians
programme.desc: A fight over the family Christmas card escalates into a major feud. Meanwhile, Scott feels guilty for moving on to a new relationship.

programme.title: Flip It Like Disick
programme.desc: With the HOA shutdown still keeping Jed Smith at a standstill, Willa and Benny renovate their offices. Scott looks for ways to make up for lost time.

programme.title: Botched
programme.desc: After a dog ripped off half her lip, a 25-year-old seeks help from the doctors. Dr Dubrow tackles a butt that's been supersized by spina bifida.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians
programme.desc: As the Kardashian Cuban holiday continues, Kim's commitment to her work engagements on Mother's Day stirs up a disagreement.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians
programme.desc: The drama between Rob and the family flares up again after Chyna launches an app. Meanwhile, Scott tries to prove that Kourtney's allergies are fake.

programme.title: E! News
programme.desc: The "E! News" team creates an often-irreverent look at Hollywood happenings, along with all the latest celebrity news, trends and gossip. The anchors and reporters cover television, film and music, as well as the celebrity party scene.

programme.title: Dating: No Filter
programme.desc: Crop top fashion designer William climbs to new heights to find love with product manager Andrew on a risky high-wire ropes course. Solomon and Atlanta-native Maya go chocolate making on their date, and the night gets sweeter from there.

programme.title: Dating: No Filter
programme.desc: Country girl Alex is looking for a devout man in Elijah, special effects makeup artist Taylor and bud-tender Lam commemorate their first date by getting sentimental tattoos together.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians
programme.desc: Khloe invites her ex to True's first birthday party. Kourtney suffers anxiety about turning 40.

programme.title: Botched

programme.title: Nightly Pop
programme.desc: This fresh and edgy late-night talk show, hosted by Morgan Stewart, Nina Parker and Hunter March, delves into the latest celebrity and pop culture news. Packed with trending entertainment news, humorous sketches and over-the-top games, this weekly show attacks top headlines and juicy gossip without the boundaries of daytime television. These three hosts exchange their unfiltered and no-holds-barred opinions on pop culture's hottest, and sometimes weirdest, stories of the week. The snarky banter and personal anecdotes often veer from the unexpected and inappropriate to the completely outrageous. These diverse, young hosts offer both weekly breakdowns and wild takedowns of everything celebrity.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Her actual name is Sandra Lee, but she is so popular in her field of dermatology that she is known by most people as Dr. Pimple Popper. In 2015, Dr. Lee began to provide a window into her job by filming dermatological procedures -- some quite gruesome, like blackhead extractions and cyst dissections -- and posting them to her website and across other social media outlets. Lo and behold, Lee gained a prominent online fan base, including a YouTube channel with more than 3 million followers called "Popaholics.' Now comes a reality show on TLC, which says Lee is a `celebrity who has pioneered the fastest growing medical fascination in decades."

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: China Open
programme.desc: The definitive guide to the markets in Hong Kong and on the mainland. Rishaad Salamat, David Ingles, Tom Mackenzie and Yvonne Man bring the latest news and analysis to start the trading day.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Asia
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets' banner, with each focusing on that geographic area"s markets. This Asian-focused version is hosted by Shery Ahn, David Ingles, Haidi Lun, Rishaad Salamat and Angie Lau. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Middle East
programme.desc: A focus on global macro issues with a Middle Eastern context.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: "Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe' joins the network's lineup to deliver viewers information about business and financial news from the region. Manus Cranny and Anna Edwards host the show from London, joined by business professionals and journalists who offer their thoughts on the day's industry happenings. This instalment of `Daybreak" joins other geographically focused versions of the show that highlight financial news in other regions.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: European Open
programme.desc: Thanks to the time difference between the locations, the financial markets in Europe open when most Americans are sleeping. Bloomberg covers the start of the European trading day with this show - which airs in the early morning hours in the Eastern time zone - as part of the network's programming block dedicated to covering financial activity overseas. The show, broadcast from London, features discussions of the stories that are expected to impact the European markets that day and, subsequently, other markets around the world.

programme.title: Bloomberg Surveillance
programme.desc: Insight on the top business stories and the state of the markets.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Americas
programme.desc: "Bloomberg Daybreak: Americas' joins the network's morning lineup to deliver viewers information about business and financial news from the region. Hosted from New York, business professionals and journalists offer their thoughts on the day's industry happenings. This instalment of `Daybreak" joins other geographically focused versions of the show that highlight financial news in other regions.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: The Open
programme.desc: Showcasing the most insightful and informative Bloomberg charts to help position the global audience for the trading day, accompanied by the Street's biggest and most respected strategists.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Americas
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets' banner, with each focusing on that geographic area"s markets. This American-focused version is hosted by Mark Barton, Scarlet Fu, David Gura and Vonnie Quinn. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: European Close
programme.desc: Thanks to the time difference between the locations, the financial markets in Europe close when the American markets are still in full swing. Bloomberg covers the end of the European trading day with this half-hour show - which airs in the morning US time, afternoon in Europe - during the network's "Bloomberg Markets' programming block. The show is hosted by two anchors on separate continents - Betty Liu in Bloomberg's New York studio and Mark Barton in London - who discuss the stories that impacted the European markets that day. The show also features sound bites from some of the day"s biggest news.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology' takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the US markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled `Bloomberg West", is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bloomberg Money Undercover
programme.desc: Bloomberg Money Undercover looks at the world of private debt, equity and real estate. Lisa Abramowicz meets investors who are pouring money into the private market at an unprecedented level.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Americas
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets' banner, with each focusing on that geographic area"s markets. This American-focused version is hosted by Mark Barton, Scarlet Fu, David Gura and Vonnie Quinn. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: The Close
programme.desc: The final minutes and seconds before the closing bell on Wall Street with the latest news, data and expert analysis.

programme.title: What'd You Miss?
programme.desc: People are busy in the fast-paced world these days and may miss some of the news in the financial world that affects their investments. Bloomberg has that covered with this daily, half-hour show anchored by Alix Steel and Joe Weisenthal. After the market closes, they analyse what they took out of the day's activities around the world and what viewers can take away from it. While the show is sophisticated enough to reach a Wall Street audience, Steel and Weisenthal also aim to make their information clear enough that even casual investors can learn something.

The show is hosted by two anchors on separate continents - Betty Liu in Bloomberg's New York studio and Mark Barton in London - who discuss the stories that impacted the European markets that day. The show also features sound bites from some of the day"s biggest news.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology' takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the US markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled `Bloomberg West", is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Not everyone has a lot of money to invest, which is why Bloomberg has financial indices that are based on fixed income. That's exactly the focus of this weekly show. It covers news and trends in the fixed-income market and includes interviews with guests from major financial institutions. "Bloomberg Real Yield" incorporates data and analysis from terminal bond experts to help dive into topics that are moving the bond markets in order to help empower viewers to make knowledgeable financial decisions.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Americas
programme.desc: In an effort to provide extensive coverage of worldwide financial markets, Bloomberg features several regional shows under the "Bloomberg Markets' banner, with each focusing on that geographic area"s markets. This American-focused version is hosted by Mark Barton, Scarlet Fu, David Gura and Vonnie Quinn. They report the latest business news and speak with the likes of political and business leaders to help investors make decisions that build their portfolios.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: Rules & Returns
programme.desc: An exploration of how the regulatory landscape changed in the aftermath of the financial crisis, and the implications and opportunities of this change.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: The Close
programme.desc: The final minutes and seconds before the closing bell on Wall Street with the latest news, data and expert analysis.

programme.title: What'd You Miss?
programme.desc: People are busy in the fast-paced world these days and may miss some of the news in the financial world that affects their investments. Bloomberg has that covered with this daily, half-hour show anchored by Alix Steel and Joe Weisenthal. After the market closes, they analyse what they took out of the day's activities around the world and what viewers can take away from it. While the show is sophisticated enough to reach a Wall Street audience, Steel and Weisenthal also aim to make their information clear enough that even casual investors can learn something.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology' takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the US markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled `Bloomberg West", is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show
programme.desc: David Rubenstein talks with his peers, leaders of some of America's biggest companies, in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader.

programme.title: Bloomberg ETF IQ
programme.desc: Scarlet Fu and Eric Balchunas are joined by leaders in this market, providing critical intelligence to financial advisers and investors of ETFs.

programme.title: Bloomberg Commodities Edge
programme.desc: The smartest voices from the commodity world discuss the biggest stories and trends in the market.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Not everyone has a lot of money to invest, which is why Bloomberg has financial indices that are based on fixed income. That's exactly the focus of this weekly show. It covers news and trends in the fixed-income market and includes interviews with guests from major financial institutions. "Bloomberg Real Yield" incorporates data and analysis from terminal bond experts to help dive into topics that are moving the bond markets in order to help empower viewers to make knowledgeable financial decisions.

programme.title: Sand Wars
programme.desc: A revealing insight into sand, one of the most consumed natural resources on the planet and the source of silicon dioxide, a mineral found in an astounding variety of everyday products. An elementary particle that is the foundation of development, it is used in many aspects of modern construction. However, the high demand for sand is putting pressure on the planet's reserves as smuggling bands, or "sand mafias", plunder beaches and rivers for this prized commodity.

programme.title: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." The show also delivers what "SportsCenter" fans are used to -- news and highlights, guests and interviews -- but in a different, fast-paced and insightful way.

programme.title: NFL PrimeTime
programme.desc: An evolution of ESPN's iconic NFL highlight show, host Trey Wingo makes sure "NFL PrimeTime" lives on in its afternoon home on ESPN. Wingo is joined by former player Tim Hasselbeck and former NFL head coach John Fox as they recap Sunday's NFL action with highlights and in-depth analysis.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: Get Up
programme.desc: Paul Finebaum and Kirk Herbstreit join Mike Greenberg and Jalen Rose to discuss and analyze the latest news from the world of sports.

programme.title: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith and Max Kellerman join host Molly Qerim Rose on this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith and Kellerman provide their expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: Outside the Lines
programme.desc: Hosts Ryan Smith and Jeremy Schaap investigate sports issues off the field. The series covers a range of sports and controversies, correspondents share inspirational or sensational stories, plus Smith and Schaap read viewer e-mails on a variety of topics.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Wendi Nix hosts Ryan Clark and Dianna Russini for an analysis-driven program focused on Xs and Os, NFL headlines, news, injuries and more.

programme.title: NBA: The Jump
programme.desc: "The Jump" is a daily NBA show hosted by Rachel Nichols. The fast-paced program originates from Los Angeles- across the street from where the Lakers and Clippers play- and features appearances by current and former players, as well as a rotating cast of ESPN analysts, reporters and insiders who provide commentary. "The Jump" is like having a fun NBA conversation with your friends in your living room, except your friends are Scottie Pippen and Tracy McGrady. The discussion encompasses all the fun, serious, smart, crazy big-issue, small-smile moments that make the NBA popular.

programme.title: High Noon
programme.desc: Bomani Jones and Pablo Torre provide their unique perspective on the biggest sports topics of the day. The two friends debate the hottest topics while also taking time to laugh at the funniest moments of the day.

programme.title: Highly Questionable
programme.desc: Sarah Spain joins Dan Le Batard and Papi to provide a questionable approach to sports, highlighting a thoughtful, irreverent and often humorous perspective on the world of professional sports.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Tony Reali hosts Ramona Shelburne, Woody Paige, Tim Cowlishaw, and J.A. Adande from across the country to banter about sports stories of the day in a competitive game format.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.desc: College football has evolved in so many transformative ways. All throughout its history, the sport's constant evolutions have had common threads, with officials and coaches striving to make it safer, more exciting, and more entertaining.

programme.desc: From Wrigley Field in Chicago.

The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: Get Up
programme.desc: Adam Schefter and Damien Woody join Mike Greenberg and Jalen Rose to discuss and analyze the latest news from the world of sports.

programme.title: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith and Max Kellerman join host Molly Qerim Rose on this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith and Kellerman provide their expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: Outside the Lines
programme.desc: Hosts Ryan Smith and Jeremy Schaap investigate sports issues off the field. The series covers a range of sports and controversies, correspondents share inspirational or sensational stories, plus Smith and Schaap read viewer e-mails on a variety of topics.

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Wendi Nix hosts Rob Ninkovich and Victor Cruz for an analysis-driven program focused on Xs and Os, NFL headlines, news, injuries and more.

programme.title: NBA: The Jump
programme.desc: "The Jump" is a daily NBA show hosted by Rachel Nichols. The fast-paced program originates from Los Angeles- across the street from where the Lakers and Clippers play- and features appearances by current and former players, as well as a rotating cast of ESPN analysts, reporters and insiders who provide commentary. "The Jump" is like having a fun NBA conversation with your friends in your living room, except your friends are Scottie Pippen and Tracy McGrady. The discussion encompasses all the fun, serious, smart, crazy big-issue, small-smile moments that make the NBA popular.

programme.title: High Noon
programme.desc: Bomani Jones and Pablo Torre provide their unique perspective on the biggest sports topics of the day. The two friends debate the hottest topics while also taking time to laugh at the funniest moments of the day.

programme.title: Highly Questionable
programme.desc: Sarah Spain joins Dan Le Batard and Papi to provide a questionable approach to sports, highlighting a thoughtful, irreverent and often humorous perspective on the world of professional sports.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Tony Reali hosts Jorge Sedano, Stugotz, Clinton Yates, and Frank Isola from across the country to banter about sports stories of the day in a competitive game format.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: College Football 150: The Greatest
programme.desc: College football has continually evolved and innovated in ways that have made the game safer, more entertaining, and more exciting. These are the Top 11 innovations that have impacted the sport's history.

programme.title: College Football Countdown
programme.desc: College football experts and insiders preview the upcoming game.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The Green Wave host the Cougars to kick off Week 4 of the college football slate. Tulane is 2-1 in 2019 after a 52-point win over Missouri State. Houston is 1-2 this year, dropping both games to ranked opponents. Houston downed Tulane 48-17 in 2018.

programme.title: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-driven reports and what's new on social media platforms.

programme.title: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." The show also delivers what "SportsCenter" fans are used to -- news and highlights, guests and interviews -- but in a different, fast-paced and insightful way.

programme.title: MLB Baseball

programme.title: High Noon
programme.desc: Bomani Jones and Pablo Torre provide their unique perspective on the biggest sports topics of the day. The two friends debate the hottest topics while also taking time to laugh at the funniest moments of the day.

programme.title: Highly Questionable
programme.desc: Veteran Miami Herald columnist and radio host Dan Le Batard leads a humorous, critical and sometimes controversial discussion of the hot sports topics of the day, from the Clevelander Hotel in Miami Beach. Known as a thoughtful and witty contrarian, Le Batard is not afraid to speak his mind and let his innate Cuban-American influences shine through, which is apparent in the fast-paced show. He's joined on each 30-minute episode by his father, Gonzalo "Papi" Le Batard.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want.

Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Jalen & Jacoby
programme.desc: NBA analyst Jalen Rose and ESPN producer David Jacoby have been "giving the people what they want" since 2011 with their fun, intelligent daily talk show on ESPN2. This show highlights the chemistry between two friends and industry trendsetters as they break down the latest action in sports, pop culture, technology and lobster.

programme.title: Golic & Wingo
programme.desc: Mike Golic and Trey Wingo bring decades of sports knowledge and entertainment to ESPN's morning drive show. Golic and Wingo deliver the news and opinion you've grown accustomed to on the Worldwide Leader. Mike Golic Jr. and Jason Fitz bring a youthful exuberance to create a fast-paced show for every sports fan. Plus, insiders and analysts add expertise to every breaking news story.

programme.title: The Dan Le Batard Show With Stugotz
programme.desc: In 2013, Dan Le Batard brought his weekday Miami radio show to ESPN's national airwaves, continuing his longstanding relationship with the sports conglomerate that began at ESPN The Magazine and morphed years later to ESPN2 as host of the popular series "Highly Questionable." Now ESPNU gets into the act by serving as the simulcast home of Le Batard's three-hour national radio show. Joined by sidekick Jon "Stugotz" Weiner, Dan offers a unique blend of self-deprecating humor, insightful guests, and thought-provoking conversation about major sports issue of the day. As he says, "we look forward to making fun of the powerful, the cliché and ourselves."

programme.title: The Stephen A. Smith Show
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith offers up his unique perspectives on the world of sports and much more.

programme.title: The Will Cain Show
programme.desc: Will Cain gives you his unfiltered take on the biggest stories in the sports world.

programme.title: Daily Wager
programme.desc: Doug Kezirian joins sports betting experts as well as ESPN analysts and reporters from various sports for discussion centered on analytics and point spreads as well as how the day's sports news affects betting.

programme.desc: Veteran Miami Herald columnist and radio host Dan Le Batard leads a humorous, critical and sometimes controversial discussion of the hot sports topics of the day, from the Clevelander Hotel in Miami Beach. Known as a thoughtful and witty contrarian, Le Batard is not afraid to speak his mind and let his innate Cuban-American influences shine through, which is apparent in the fast-paced show. He's joined on each 30-minute episode by his father, Gonzalo "Papi" Le Batard.

programme.title: Around the Horn
programme.desc: Hosted by Tony Reali in the ESPN studio and joined via satellite by four panelists from across the country, the team debates popular sports stories. Reali acts as moderator, giving points for well-made arguments and deducting points for those who inspire him to hit the "mute" button. At the end of the show, whomever Reali deems the winner has 30 seconds without interruption to voice their final opinions on any topic they want.

programme.title: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Golic & Wingo
programme.desc: Mike Golic and Trey Wingo bring decades of sports knowledge and entertainment to ESPN's morning drive show. Golic and Wingo deliver the news and opinion you've grown accustomed to on the Worldwide Leader. Mike Golic Jr. and Jason Fitz bring a youthful exuberance to create a fast-paced show for every sports fan. Plus, insiders and analysts add expertise to every breaking news story.

programme.title: The Dan Le Batard Show With Stugotz
programme.desc: In 2013, Dan Le Batard brought his weekday Miami radio show to ESPN's national airwaves, continuing his longstanding relationship with the sports conglomerate that began at ESPN The Magazine and morphed years later to ESPN2 as host of the popular series "Highly Questionable." Now ESPNU gets into the act by serving as the simulcast home of Le Batard's three-hour national radio show. Joined by sidekick Jon "Stugotz" Weiner, Dan offers a unique blend of self-deprecating humor, insightful guests, and thought-provoking conversation about major sports issue of the day. As he says, "we look forward to making fun of the powerful, the cliché and ourselves."

programme.title: The Stephen A. Smith Show
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith offers up his unique perspectives on the world of sports and much more.

programme.title: The Will Cain Show
programme.desc: Will Cain gives you his unfiltered take on the biggest stories in the sports world.

programme.title: Daily Wager
programme.desc: Doug Kezirian joins sports betting experts as well as ESPN analysts and reporters from various sports for discussion centered on analytics and point spreads as well as how the day's sports news affects betting.

programme.title: NBA: The Jump
programme.desc: "The Jump" is a daily NBA show hosted by Rachel Nichols. The fast-paced program originates from Los Angeles- across the street from where the Lakers and Clippers play- and features appearances by current and former players, as well as a rotating cast of ESPN analysts, reporters and insiders who provide commentary. "The Jump" is like having a fun NBA conversation with your friends in your living room, except your friends are Scottie Pippen and Tracy McGrady. The discussion encompasses all the fun, serious, smart, crazy big-issue, small-smile moments that make the NBA popular.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The Cowboys take on a state rival in the Golden Hurricane. Oklahoma State blasted past Oregon State and McNeese to begin 2019, scoring 108 points across the two contests. Oklahoma State has scored 59 points in two straight victories over Tulsa.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The undefeated Broncos and freshman quarterback Hank Bachmeier host the FCS Vikings. Bachmeier guided Boise State (2-0) to victories at Florida State and over Marshall. Portland State (1-1) suffered a season-opening setback at Arkansas 20-13.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The Bearcats face an in-state rival for the second week in a row when they host the RedHawks. Cincinnati has won 13 straight over Miami (Ohio), the longest streak in this rivalry's history, and has maintained its iron grip on the Victory Bell.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The undefeated No. 25 Cavaliers and Bryce Perkins host the Seminoles. Perkins has accounted for 485 total yards and five scores in 2019. Florida State (1-1) beat Louisiana-Monroe in overtime after losing its opener to then-No. 22 Boise State.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The top-ranked Tigers visit the Orange in an ACC clash. Clemson (2-0) and Syracuse (1-1) have split the last two meetings by a combined seven points. The Tigers' Travis Etienne has gained 9.2 yards per carry and rushed for three TDs this season.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 17 Knights head home to host the Cardinal. Central Florida has been dominant to begin 2019, outscoring foes 110-14. Three different quarterbacks have tossed a TD for UCF, with Dillon Gabriel's five leading the way. Stanford is 1-1 this year.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 13 Nittany Lions host a state rival in the Panthers. Penn State has opened up 2019 with wins over Idaho and Buffalo, scoring 124 points across the two tilts. Since the head-to-head series resumed in 2016, Penn State is 2-1 versus Pittsburgh.

programme.title: College Football Live
programme.desc: Want to know up-to-the-minute reports about your favorite college football team? The half-hour program features a rotating roster of analysts that includes coaches, former players, and media personalities.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The WIldcats (2-0) kick off conference play against the ninth-ranked Gators (2-0) in Week 3. Kentucky snapped a 31-game losing skid against Florida with a 27-16 victory last year. That win was Kentucky's first since 1986 against its SEC rival.

programme.title: WNBA Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 1 Mystics host the No. 4 Aces in Game 1 of this WNBA semifinal series. Dearica Hamby's late steal followed by a 3-pointer sealed a win for Las Vegas over Chicago in the single-knockout second round. Washington earned a bye to this point.

programme.title: NFL Football
programme.desc: The Browns look to rebound as they face the Jets on Monday Night Football. Baker Mayfield (CLE) threw three interceptions while taking five sacks in a Week 1 loss against Tennessee. New York quarterback Sam Darnold will miss time with mononucleosis.

programme.title: Good Morning Football
programme.desc: NFL Network enters the morning show fray with a three-hour live presentation. Each day from Manhattan, "Good Morning Football" brings viewers up-to-the-minute breaking NFL news and local NFL stories on a national platform. Led by host Kay Adams, the show features a combination of headline-making interviews and a discussion of a wide array of issues in the areas of sports, news and entertainment. Executive producer Michael Davies ("Watch What Happens Live," Jerry Seinfeld's "Comedians in Cars Getting Coffee") says by booking celebrity guests and opinionated national and local journalists, and taking advantage of a close collaboration with all 32 NFL franchises, "Good Morning Football" should break out within and well beyond its time period.

programme.desc: The Browns look to rebound as they face the Jets on Monday Night Football. Baker Mayfield (CLE) threw three interceptions while taking five sacks in a Week 1 loss against Tennessee. New York quarterback Sam Darnold will miss time with mononucleosis.

programme.title: NFL Fantasy Live
programme.desc: Millions of NFL fans live in a fantasy world, where survival depends on successfully navigating the twists and turns of a grueling season. After all, relying on a second-string quarterback or an untested wide receiver to come up big is usually foolhardy. Avoiding such predicaments takes plenty of preparation, and that's where "NFL Fantasy Live" comes into play. Hosts Matt "Money" Smith and James Koh provide the advice and analysis fantasy fans need to make winning lineup decisions. And being armed with the latest player updates, stats and trends goes a long way toward turning fantasies into championships.

programme.title: NFL Total Access
programme.desc: The NFL Network's flagship program airs six nights a week, churning out the day's top headlines and much more covering all 32 teams. The show also provides fans with exclusive footage from inside team facilities and interviews with players and coaches. Joining hosts Dan Hellie and Amber Theoharis with analysis and insight are a deep roster of NFL Network personnel plus other former players as guest analysts throughout each week.

programme.title: NFL Football
programme.desc: The Cowboys visit the Redskins in an NFC East duel. Dak Prescott (DAL) was near-perfect in Week 1, tossing for 405 yards with four TD passes in a 35-17 triumph over division rival New York. Dallas has won nine of the last 12 meetings with Washington.

Executive producer Michael Davies ("Watch What Happens Live," Jerry Seinfeld's "Comedians in Cars Getting Coffee") says by booking celebrity guests and opinionated national and local journalists, and taking advantage of a close collaboration with all 32 NFL franchises, "Good Morning Football" should break out within and well beyond its time period.

programme.title: NFL Now
programme.desc: Matt "Money" Smith goes inside the NFL media newsroom for all the latest NFL news, injury updates and inside information from reporters across the country.

programme.title: NFL Now
programme.desc: Matt "Money" Smith goes inside the NFL media newsroom for all the latest NFL news, injury updates and inside information from reporters across the country.

programme.title: TNF First Look
programme.desc: Prepping the viewer for tonight's game, plus inside access from reporters across the country.

programme.title: NFL Fantasy Live
programme.desc: Millions of NFL fans live in a fantasy world, where survival depends on successfully navigating the twists and turns of a grueling season. After all, relying on a second-string quarterback or an untested wide receiver to come up big is usually foolhardy. Avoiding such predicaments takes plenty of preparation, and that's where "NFL Fantasy Live" comes into play. Hosts Matt "Money" Smith and James Koh provide the advice and analysis fantasy fans need to make winning lineup decisions. And being armed with the latest player updates, stats and trends goes a long way toward turning fantasies into championships.

programme.title: NFL Football
programme.desc: AFC South squads aiming to rebound from losses collide as Jacksonville hosts Tennessee. The Titans mustered just 242 yards of total offense in a 19-17 loss to the Colts in Week 2. The Titans have won each of their last four meetings with the Jaguars.

programme.title: Thursday Night Football Postgame Show
programme.desc: Airing live on NFL Network, the postgame coverage of "Thursday Night Football" features Colleen Wolfe, Steve Smith Sr., Michael Irvin and Joe Thomas providing highlights, analysis and interviews. Each week, a player or coach from the winning team comes on-set to talk about the game's key moments. The guest also receives a gift or two from the crew.

programme.title: NFL Total Access
programme.desc: Lindsay Rhodes and Scott Hanson provide the latest NFL news, opinions, highlights and interviews from around the league.

programme.title: NFL 100 Greatest
programme.desc: NFL stars, legends and celebrities help count down the 100th to 61st greatest plays in NFL history.

programme.title: NFL 100 Greatest
programme.desc: NFL stars, legends and celebrities help count down the 60th to 31st greatest plays in NFL history.

programme.desc: The NFL Network's flagship program airs six nights a week, churning out the day's top headlines and much more covering all 32 teams. programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 1 of the 1992 NBA Finals. Michael Jordan hits six 3-pointers to set an NBA Finals record. From June 3, 1992.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 2 of the 1993 NBA Finals. Michael Jordan and Charles Barkley put on a show with both scoring 42 points each. From June 11, 1993.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 6 of the 1993 NBA Finals. John Paxson hits the game-winning 3-pointer to help the Bulls win their third straight NBA title. From June 20, 1993.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Michael Jordan scores 26 points as the Bulls cruise to their NBA-record 72nd win. They finish the season going 72-10, three games better than the previous best record. From April 21, 1996.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 2 of the 1996 NBA Finals. Dennis Rodman scores 10 points and grabs 20 rebounds to help the bulls beat the Sonics. From June 7, 1996.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 5 of the 1997 NBA Finals. Known as "The Flu Game," Michael Jordan overcomes an illness to score 38 points and lead the Bulls to victory over the Jazz. From June 11, 1997.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 6 of the 1997 NBA Finals. Steve Kerr becomes the hero of the game after hitting the game-winning shot to help the Bulls win their 5th championship. From June 13, 1997.

programme.title: Hardwood Classics
programme.desc: Game 6 of the 1998 NBA Finals. Michael Jordan hits the game-winning shot over Bryon Russell to give Chicago their 6th NBA title. From June 14, 1998.

programme.title: Tin Cup
programme.desc: Roy McAvoy (Kevin Costner) was a golf pro with a bright future, but his rebellious nature and bad attitude cost him everything. Now working as a golf instructor, he falls for his newest pupil, Dr. Molly Griswold (Rene Russo), a psychiatrist who happens to be the girlfriend of PGA Tour star and Roy's rival, David Simms (Don Johnson). After he is humiliated by Simms at a celebrity golf tournament, McAvoy decides to make a run for the PGA Tour, as well as Molly's heart.

programme.title: Time to Schein
programme.desc: Since talking sports during his undergrad days on the Syracuse University airwaves, Adam Schein's passionate, energetic, fan-friendly style has served him well, from the legendary WFAN in New York to a daily show on Sirius Radio. It's also prepared him for his dream job -- "Time to Schein," a national weekday series on CBS Sports Network. In a fast-moving, unscripted format, the host presents his unique perspective on the latest news and social media trends, provides bold predictions and interacts with key players, personalities and league personnel. Schein also hosts "That Other Pregame Show" and "NFL Monday QB" on CBS Sports Network during the NFL season.

programme.desc: There are smart, talented women to be heard in male-dominated sports TV. CBS Sports Network tries to level the field with "We Need to Talk," a groundbreaking series hosted, produced, directed by women. A rotating panel includes Pro Football Hall of Fame sportscaster Lesley Visser, celebrated journalist Andrea Kremer, CBS Sports Radio host Dana Jacobson, world champion boxer Laila Ali, and CBS college football reporter Allie LaForce. Their guests are athletes, TV/radio personalities, league/team executives, and news/sports journalists. Coordinating producer Suzanne Smith hopes the show provides girls with great role models in sports journalism.

Danger lurks on the horizon as Leon Rom (Christoph Waltz), a treacherous envoy for King Leopold, devises a scheme that lures the couple to the Congo. Rom plans to capture Tarzan and deliver him to an old enemy in exchange for diamonds. When Jane becomes a pawn in his devious plot, Tarzan must return to the jungle to save the woman he loves.

With the help of his best friend (Ben Foster), Chris assembles a team to run to Panama to retrieve a fortune in counterfeit bills. When things go awry, Chris must call on his rusty skills to complete the task before his family pays the price.

As the pandemic threatens to consume humanity, Gerry leads a worldwide search to find the source of the infection and, with luck, a way to halt its spread.

programme.title: Independence Day: Resurgence

Fortunately, 20 years earlier, nations across the world started to use recovered extraterrestrial technology to develop an immense defense program. When the alien invaders attack with unprecedented force, the U.S. president, teams of scientists and brave fighter pilots spring into action to save the planet from a seemingly invincible enemy.

programme.desc: Mike and Dave Stangle are young, adventurous, fun-loving brothers who tend to get out of control at family gatherings. When Nick and his partner come under attack in the Middle East, the ensuing battle accidentally unearths Ahmanet, a betrayed Egyptian princess who was entombed under the desert for thousands of years. With her powers constantly evolving, Morton must now stop the resurrected monster as she embarks on a furious rampage through the streets of London.

programme.title: Deadpool
programme.desc: Wade Wilson (Ryan Reynolds) is a former Special Forces operative who now works as a mercenary. His world comes crashing down when evil scientist Ajax (Ed Skrein) tortures, disfigures and transforms him into Deadpool. The rogue experiment leaves Deadpool with accelerated healing powers and a twisted sense of humor. With help from mutant allies Colossus and Negasonic Teenage Warhead (Brianna Hildebrand), Deadpool uses his new skills to hunt down the man who nearly destroyed his life.

programme.desc: Ten years after simian flu wiped out much of the world's homosapiens, genetically enhanced chimpanzee Caesar (Andy Serkis) and his ever-growing band of followers have established a thriving colony just outside San Francisco in Muir Woods. Meanwhile, a small band of human survivors emerges, which forces Caesar -- as leader -- to grapple with the dual challenge of protecting his people and re-establishing a relationship with the remaining human population -- the latter being Caesar's secret wish.

programme.title: Alien: Covenant
programme.desc: Bound for a remote planet on the far side of the galaxy, members (Katherine Waterston, Billy Crudup) of the colony ship Covenant discover what they think to be an uncharted paradise. While there, they meet David (Michael Fassbender), the synthetic survivor of the doomed Prometheus expedition. The mysterious world soon turns dark and dangerous when a hostile alien life-form forces the crew into a deadly fight for survival.

programme.title: The Great Wall
programme.desc: When a mercenary warrior (Matt Damon) is imprisoned within the Great Wall, he discovers the mystery behind one of the greatest wonders of the world. As wave after wave of marauding beasts besiege the massive structure, his quest for fortune turns into a journey toward heroism as he joins a huge army of elite warriors to confront the unimaginable and seemingly unstoppable force.

programme.title: Let's Be Cops
programme.desc: Best pals Ryan (Jake Johnson) and Justin (Damon Wayans Jr.) are stalled in their respective careers -- a fact that is painfully driven home when they go to a college reunion. Dressed as police in the mistaken belief that they were to attend a costume party, Ryan and Justin find that the uniforms earn them much respect and attention. Although Justin is uncomfortable with the idea, Ryan decides to continue with the charade, putting them both in increasingly dangerous situations.

Peter must soon put his powers to the test when the evil Vulture emerges to threaten everything that he holds dear.

Helen retreats to the house of her grandmother Madea (Tyler Perry), who helps her destroy much of Charles' property, earning her house arrest. While Charles prepares for the trial of a corrupt client, Helen is courted by Orlando (Shemar Moore), an affectionate moving man with strong Christian values.

programme.title: Wife Swap
